problem_when_combined: Layering a retinoid with an AHA in the same routine compounds exfoliation and commonly causes irritation and a compromised barrier
recommendation: Alternate on different nights rather than stacking

problem_when_combined: Retinoids with BHA in the same routine increase irritation and dryness for most people
recommendation: Use the BHA in the morning or on alternate nights

problem_when_combined: Benzoyl peroxide oxidises and deactivates many retinoids (notably tretinoin) when applied together, reducing efficacy
recommendation: Apply at different times of day (e.g. BPO AM

problem_when_combined: Benzoyl peroxide can oxidise pure vitamin C (ascorbic acid) applied at the same time
column6: Separate into AM and PM routines

problem_when_combined: Combining an AHA and a BHA in the same step doubles up exfoliating acids and frequently over-exfoliates skin
recommendation: Limit to one acid per routine or alternate days

problem_when_combined: Using two different retinoid products together multiplies strength and the risk of retinoid dermatitis
Stacking an AHA with benzoyl peroxide is a strong combined irritant load and can over-dry the skin
recommendation: Alternate nights and buffer with moisturiser
